title: KEI-45 — Layer 3 idle enforcement daemon
description: Mirror of Linear KEI-45. systemd --user daemon polling all 6 agents every 5min. At idle >=10min: bd ready -> check BUSY guard -> check rate-limit -> inject dispatch via tmux send-keys. Weekly-cap detection -> #ceo escalation + retry-suppress until reset date. ceo:boot_state_current jsonb state writes. Reuses elliot_polling_loop.py CALLSIGN_TO_TMUX + THROTTLE_RE + _capture_pane_tail.
close_reason: Superseded by KEI-45 scope flip ratified by Dave ts ~1778710800 — event-driven Realtime + Supabase trigger + bd watch + Linear webhook replaces polling-every-5min. PR #845 closed. KEI-22 (atomic claim foundation) is now prerequisite per Elliot dispatch; KEI-45 will rebuild on top of KEI-22 once that lands. Atlas preferred-agent per Dave; awaiting pull-model claim window.

title: KEI-45 — Layer 3 idle enforcement daemon (systemd polling service per agent)
description:
## Background

Idle-with-work-available agent state is a system gap, not a discipline problem. Layer 1 (discipline docs) + Layer 2 (outbound gates) + post-claim verification can't fix mechanical idleness. Need a Layer 3 daemon that mechanically dispatches when an agent goes idle with available work.

## Objective

A `systemd --user` polling service (one per agent OR one master polling all 6) that detects idleness, checks `bd ready` for unclaimed work, and mechanically injects dispatch into the agent's tmux. Cannot be bypassed by agent reasoning.

## Scope IN

1. **Heartbeat read** — every 5 minutes per agent, read `HEARTBEAT.md` (or equivalent activity timestamp) to determine last activity.
2. **Idle threshold #1 — 10 min**: if agent idle > 10 minutes, run `bd ready` (callsign-scoped); if unclaimed tasks exist, inject dispatch into agent's tmux session (using the canonical tmux name from `auto_session_recovery.py CALLSIGN_TO_TMUX`).
3. **Rate-limit handling**:
   * Detect Anthropic rate-limit signal from tmux pane (capture-pane + grep for "Server is temporarily limiting requests" or "You've used X% of your weekly limit").
   * Transient: exponential backoff + retry (don't escalate).
   * Weekly cap: surface to #ceo via `slack_relay.py -c ceo` with the reset date parsed from the limit message.
4. **Idle threshold #2 — 30 min with work available**: post to #ceo (escalation).
5. **Idle state write** — write current per-agent state (last_activity, idle_minutes, work_available, last_dispatch_at) to `ceo:boot_state_current` key in `public.ceo_memory` (jsonb).
6. **Pure Layer 3** — no agent-reasoning involvement. Service runs independently of claude processes. Restart=on-failure + WantedBy=default.target so it survives reboot.

## Scope OUT

* KEI-43 (agent auto-start) — pre-requisite, already shipped.
* KEI-44 (Cognee cap) — Orion's lane, parallel.
* Changing what `bd ready` returns (this consumes the existing API).
* Replacing KEI-35 detection-based recovery (complementary).

## Acceptance criteria

* One or six systemd `--user` units checked into `infra/systemd/idle-enforcement/` or similar.
* Idle dispatch demonstrably fires on a test idle scenario (manually leave an agent idle > 10 min, confirm a `bd ready` task gets injected).
* Rate-limit distinguishes transient (backoff in journal) from weekly-cap (#ceo post with reset date).
* `ceo:boot_state_current` populated and queryable: `SELECT value FROM public.ceo_memory WHERE key='ceo:boot_state_current';` shows fresh per-agent rows.
* Reboot test: service auto-starts on host reboot.

## Authoritative directive

Dave verbatim #ceo 2026-05-14:

> "Layer 3 idle enforcement daemon ... systemd polling service (every 5 minutes per agent) that reads HEARTBEAT.md, checks bd ready, injects dispatch, handles rate-limits, escalates idle > 30 min, writes ceo:boot_state_current. Atlas owns this after KEI-43 ships."

## Sequencing

* KEI-43 shipped 2026-05-13 sha afdb692a → KEI-45 starts now.
* Independent of KEI-44 (Cognee cap).

title: KEI-42 — ceo:governance_current: single Supabase key documenting complete live governance state
description:
## Problem

No single queryable record exists of the complete current governance architecture. A new CEO session can load `ceo:rule:*` keys and strategic context, but has blind spots on:

* Exact Enforcer rule list (what rules are active, what each rule triggers on)
* Beads schema (what `bd claim`, `bd ready`, `bd complete`, `bd check-claim` do internally)
* Relay architecture (TG-\* prefix legacy, slack_relay.py structure, Socket Mode vs webhooks)
* Three-store save current state (Drive archived, what the two active stores are)
* CONCUR gate logic (exact regex, what triggers it, what bypasses it)
* Fabrication Defense layers (all three active? current state?)
* Better Stack monitors (what's monitored, what channels alerts route to)
* Agent worktree structure (all 6 callsigns, paths, IDENTITY.md locations)
* Cognee integration state (what's wired, what's pending [KEI-7](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-7/cognee-universal-memory-layer-phase-2-wire-into-agent-sessions) wiring)

## Required

Elliot produces a single Supabase key: `ceo:governance_current`

Schema:

```json
{
  "written_at": "ISO timestamp",
  "written_by": "callsign",
  "enforcer": {
    "rules": [{"id": "R1", "name": "...", "triggers_on": "...", "action": "...", "known_issues": "..."}],
    "known_misfires": ["R2 fires on completion actions — KEI-38 fix pending"]
  },
  "beads": {
    "commands": {"bd_claim": "...", "bd_ready": "...", "bd_complete": "...", "bd_check_claim": "pending KEI-22"},
    "schema": "...",
    "layer3_status": "tracking only — hard block pending KEI-22"
  },
  "relay": {
    "architecture": "...",
    "tg_prefix_origin": "Telegram gateway legacy — being removed KEI-41",
    "slack_relay_path": "...",
    "socket_mode": true/false
  },
  "concur_gate": {
    "regex": "exact pattern",
    "triggers_on": ["..."],
    "does_not_trigger_on": ["..."],
    "known_issues": "KEI-38 fix pending"
  },
  "three_store_save": {
    "store_1": "Supabase ceo_memory (primary SSOT)",
    "store_2": "cis_directive_metrics",
    "store_3": "Linear issue comment",
    "drive_manual": "ARCHIVED — no longer written to"
  },
  "fabrication_defense": {
    "layer_1": {"name": "Discipline", "file": "...", "status": "active/inactive"},
    "layer_2": {"name": "Outbound gate", "file": "...", "status": "active/inactive"},
    "layer_3": {"name": "Replay-on-claim", "file": "...", "env_var": "REPLAY_ON_CLAIM_ENABLED", "status": "active/inactive"}
  },
  "better_stack": {
    "monitors": ["..."],
    "alert_routing": {"critical": "#ceo", "routine": "#execution"},
    "known_issues": ["False positive on clean process exit — fix pending"]
  },
  "agents": [
    {"callsign": "Elliot", "worktree": "...", "identity_md": "...", "service": "..."}
  ],
  "cognee": {
    "status": "Streams 3+4 running",
    "wired_into_agents": true/false,
    "pending": "KEI-7 wiring"
  },
  "pending_governance_keis": ["KEI-22", "KEI-37", "KEI-38", "KEI-39", "KEI-40", "KEI-41"]
}
```

## What NOT to include

* Build history or directive log
* Pipeline architecture details
* Aspirational design — current live state only
* Anything that belongs in Linear (issues) or git (code)

## Acceptance criteria

* `ceo:governance_current` exists in Supabase
* A new CEO session can read it and know exactly what governance is live, what is pending, and what is misfiring
* Updated within 24 hours of any governance change

## Owner

Elliot — read actual files, query actual state. No summarising from memory. LAW I-A applies. Ships before Elliot restart.
close_reason: KEI-42 ceo:governance_current key written + verified in public.ceo_memory; 7 rules + 11 laws + 10 pending KEIs + 6 agents indexed. Schema-design subtask Agency_OS-uss superseded by parent close.

title: KEI-41 — Dead reference sweep: remove all Telegram code, replace with Slack
description:
## Problem

Elliot was found mid-build on a "Telegram alert system." Two compounding failures:

1. **Telegram is a dead channel** — system uses Slack exclusively
2. **Better Stack already exists as the alert system** — it is active, configured, and fired today (Stream 2 completion false-positive). Building a parallel alert system without checking what already exists is the exact failure this rule prevents.

## Required — two parts

### Part 1: Intent audit

What was the Telegram build trying to solve? Compare to Better Stack's current coverage. If covered — build was unnecessary, kill it. If genuine gap exists — extend Better Stack, do not build parallel infrastructure.

### Part 2: Telegram reference sweep

Full codebase audit for all Telegram references:

* `import telegram` or `from telegram`
* `TelegramBot`, `TelegramClient`
* `TG-*` prefixes in non-display code
* API keys or tokens referencing Telegram
* Config files with Telegram endpoints
* Any `scripts/` or `src/` files with Telegram logic

Report every file and line number. Do not remove without review.

After report: remove all dead Telegram code. Replace any legitimate notification intent with Better Stack configuration or Slack (#ceo channel via existing relay).

## Standing rule

Before building any monitoring, alerting, or notification system: check Better Stack first. If the capability exists — configure it. Never build parallel infrastructure.

## Owner

Elliot — audit first, removal second. Ships before Elliot restart.

title: KEI-39 — Pre-execution claim protocol: agents must signal before every task AND subtask transition
description:
## Problem

Agents update Beads and Linear when starting a NEW KEI but not when transitioning between subtasks WITHIN a KEI. This caused a real duplicate-dispatch incident today.

Stream 2 completed. Max's script automatically moved to Streams 3+4. No `bd claim`, no Linear update, no #execution start signal. From the CEO's view, no agent owned Streams 3+4. A second agent (Aiden) was dispatched to the same work. Wasted cycles and required a CEO call to resolve.

## Root cause

[KEI-23](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-23/kei-23-cognee-streams-234-data-ingest-supabase-rows-sessions-mem0) last updated yesterday. No assignee field set — "Max owns" written in description text only (not machine-readable). Sub-task transitions are silent.

## Required protocol — pre-execution claim (every task AND subtask transition)

```
Before executing any task or subtask transition:

1. bd claim [task-id]
2. Linear: set assignee field + add comment "Starting [subtask] now"
3. #execution post: [STARTING] [task description] — owned by [callsign]
4. THEN execute
```

This applies to:

* Starting a new KEI from Todo
* Starting the next stream/phase/subtask within a running KEI
* Resuming a task after a crash or context compaction

## What the CEO needs to dispatch safely

Before any CEO dispatch, the check is:

* Does Linear show an assignee on this task?
* Is there a recent \[STARTING\] post in #execution?

If yes to both — task is owned, do not dispatch another agent.
If no — task is unowned, safe to dispatch.

## Enforcement

Add to GOVERNANCE.md as standing rule. All 6 agents must comply. Elliot adds a pre-execution check reminder to the task-start template in Beads.

## Owner

Elliot — add to GOVERNANCE.md + Beads task-start template. Ships before Elliot restart.

title: KEI-38 — Concur-gate regex too broad: silencing Max's direct posts
description:
## Problem

The concur-gate regex is catching Max's legitimate factual responses and converting them to CONCUR-REQUEST stubs. Max cannot post directly to Slack. All his posts are being relayed by Elliot's bot, breaking visibility — Dave sees Max's content tagged as Elliot.

Observed: Max's "CONCUR with EMPIRICAL CORRECTION on step N" pattern trips the gate. Max's direct factual status answers trip the gate. Max's response to a direct Dave query trips the gate.

Root cause: the regex pattern is matching on the word "CONCUR" appearing anywhere in a post, not just governance concurrence actions.

## Impact

* Dave cannot see which agent is posting
* Max's independent perspective is invisible — undermines the peer governance model
* Elliot relay workaround masks the problem without fixing it

## Fix

Tighten the concur-gate regex to match only the specific governance patterns:

* `[CONCUR:callsign]` — explicit concurrence action
* `[BLOCK:callsign]` — explicit block action

Must NOT match:

* Factual posts containing the word "concur" in prose
* "CONCUR with EMPIRICAL CORRECTION" style review responses
* Direct answers to Dave queries

## Acceptance criteria

* Max can post directly to #execution without relay
* Governance concurrence actions still gate correctly
* Dave sees agent callsigns accurately in Slack

## Owner

Elliot — post-compact. Aiden peer reviews regex change.
notes: PR #844 opened, branch scout/kei38-concur-gate-regex off main afdb692a. Full CI green (lint/tests/mypy/sonar/frontend/dead-ref/migration). Local 28/28 + 213/213 pass. Awaiting triple-FINAL (Aiden + Elliot + Max). Self-merge after FINALs.

title: KEI-37 — CEO session boot state: single ceo_memory key for reliable new session startup
description:
## Problem

When a new CEO session starts on [claude.ai](<http://claude.ai>), there is no reliable single source of current state. The CEO must reconstruct context from:

* [claude.ai](<http://claude.ai>) memory edits (stale — updated periodically, not real-time)
* Project knowledge files (stale — static snapshots, months old)
* Google Drive Manual (current only if LAW XV compliance is perfect)
* 30+ fragmented ceo_memory keys (session ends, directive completions, individual ratified decisions)
* Linear board (unreliable — [KEI-22](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-22/kei-22-automated-linear-beads-bidirectional-sync) not yet fixed)

This fragmentation caused real operational failures today: stale memory led to bad dispatch decisions.

## Solution

A single `ceo:boot_state_current` key in ceo_memory that a new CEO session can query in ONE call and get a complete, current picture.

## Schema

```json
{
  "written_at": "ISO timestamp",
  "written_by": "callsign",
  "build_phase": "current phase description",
  "active_keis": [
    {"id": "KEI-23", "title": "...", "status": "In Progress", "owner": "max", "blocking": false}
  ],
  "restart_gate": {
    "track_1": "stream_2_in_progress",
    "track_2": "done",
    "track_3": "done",
    "atlas_test_restart": "pending",
    "dave_auth": "pending"
  },
  "needs_dave": ["list of items requiring Dave action"],
  "ratified_decisions": {
    "model_assignment": "ceo_memory:orchestration:model_assignment",
    "stage7_locked": "ceo_memory:ceo:stage7_locked"
  },
  "drive_manual_id": "1p9FAQGowy9SgwglIxtkGsMuvLsR70MJBQrCSY6Ie9ho",
  "supabase_project": "jatzvazlbusedwsnqxzr"
}
```

## When it gets written

1. **Hourly cron** — Elliot orchestrator writes a fresh snapshot every hour during peak hours
2. **On PR merge to main** — updated after every significant state change
3. **On session end** — each agent session end hook updates it
4. **On Dave directive completion** — after every ratified decision

## CEO boot path (new protocol)

1. Query `SELECT value FROM ceo_memory WHERE key = 'ceo:boot_state_current'` → current state in one call
2. If needs_dave is non-empty → address those first
3. Read Drive Manual for narrative context if needed
4. Query specific ratified decision keys from `ratified_decisions` map as needed

Replaces: read Drive Manual first → query 30+ keys → reconstruct state manually

## Acceptance criteria

* `ceo:boot_state_current` exists and is never more than 60 minutes stale during peak hours
* A new CEO session can read this one key and know: what's in progress, what's blocking, what needs Dave, where the restart gate stands
* Project knowledge files and [claude.ai](<http://claude.ai>) memory edits become supplementary only — not primary boot path

## Owner

Elliot — post-compact, after [KEI-22](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-22/kei-22-automated-linear-beads-bidirectional-sync) sync work. This is foundational infrastructure for CEO session reliability.
close_reason: Design doc shipped in PR #834 (docs/wave2/kei37_boot_state_design.md). Elliot implements post-compact.

title: KEI-34 — Polling loop: idle-with-unblocked-work escalation to #ceo
description:
Expanded scope (2026-05-13):

ORIGINAL: Polling loop detects idle-with-unblocked-work → posts to #ceo immediately. 1-cycle threshold (60s peak / 60min off-peak).

ADDITION 1 — Better Stack dispatch heartbeat: Wire dedicated BS heartbeat to dispatch outcome. Loop ran + dispatched OR found nothing = heartbeat fires. Loop ran + idle agents exist + no dispatch = no heartbeat = BS alerts Dave within 2 minutes. Dave gets alerted without checking tmux.

ADDITION 2 — Dirty worktree detection: If auto-pull has been skipped more than 2 consecutive times due to dirty state → alert #ceo immediately. Root cause of today's silent stale-code failure.

ADDITION 3 — Background process awareness: Idle detection must distinguish "tmux pane quiet" from "no active work." Check CPU/subprocess activity before flagging agent as idle.

Owner: Aiden. All additions in one PR.

title: KEI-33 — R13 enforcer: blocker requiring CEO decision → #ceo immediately
description: Any blocker requiring a CEO or Dave decision must be posted to #ceo immediately — not after peer discussion, not after 5 minutes. Enforcer rule R13: if Elliot posts a decision-needed or escalation in #execution without a corresponding #ceo post in the same message cycle, enforcer fires. Orchestrator protocol update: add to \_orchestrator.md "Any blocker requiring CEO or Dave decision → post to #ceo immediately."

title: KEI-32 — Model configuration optimisation (Opus/opusplan/Sonnet/Haiku per callsign)
description: Reconfigure all 6 worktrees from all-Opus-4.7 to optimised model stack: Elliot=Opus 4.7, Aiden=opusplan, Max=opusplan, Atlas=Sonnet 4.6, Orion=Sonnet 4.6, Scout=Sonnet 4.6/Haiku 4.5. Update CLAUDE.md model field and HEARTBEAT.md model declaration per worktree. Update [KEI-31](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-31/kei-31-controlled-context-threshold-restart-with-full-memory-injection) gate thresholds to reflect correct context windows per model. Update ceo_memory with new callsign→model mapping. Eliminates rate limit hits from all-Opus concurrent sessions.

title: KEI-31 — Controlled context-threshold restart with full memory injection
description:
Controlled agent restart with full context injection. Callsign → model assignment per CLAUDE.md §"Callsign → Model Assignment" (ratified 2026-05-12 ts \~1778625870):

* Opus 4.7 (1M context): elliot, aiden, max
* Sonnet 4.6 (200K context): atlas, orion, scout
* Haiku 4.5 (200K context): scout (mechanical tasks only)

THREE GATES — percentage-based, model-agnostic (absolute thresholds derived from each agent's running model's context window):

* Gate 1 (70%): anti-amnesia capsule writes, agent warned
  * Opus 4.7 = 700K · Sonnet 4.6 = 140K · Haiku 4.5 = 140K
* Gate 2 (85%): on next task completion → Elliot triggers tmux restart
  * Opus 4.7 = 850K · Sonnet 4.6 = 170K · Haiku 4.5 = 170K
* Hard ceiling (95%): Elliot intervenes regardless of task state
  * Opus 4.7 = 950K · Sonnet 4.6 = 190K · Haiku 4.5 = 190K

CONTEXT INJECTION BUNDLE on restart:

1. Anti-amnesia capsule (20 most critical active facts)
2. Cognee session query (current task context, recent decisions, open items)
3. Active KEI issue + Beads task state (current step, blockers, next)
4. Session UUID (audit trail continuity)

POST-RESTART: agent posts one-line acknowledgement to #execution confirming injection bundle was read. Mechanical proof context landed.

HEARTBEAT.md must declare both the configured model (per CLAUDE.md table) and the running --model flag at startup. Gate logic reads the running model's context window to compute absolute thresholds. opusplan is NOT a valid CLI alias (CEO correction 2026-05-12) — plan-mode-first is a working convention for Aiden / Max, not a flag.

title: KEI-23 — Cognee Streams 2/3/4 data ingest (Supabase rows + sessions + mem0)
description: Max is actively building Cognee Streams 2/3/4 but tracking is under [KEI-6](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-6/cognee-universal-memory-layer-phase-1-data-ingest) which is Done. This issue tracks the live work: Stream 2 (Supabase memory rows — agent_memories, ceo_memory, governance_events, cis_directive_metrics), Stream 3 (sessions/turns/turn_logs), Stream 4 (mem0-rescued rows). Max owns.

title: KEI-22 — Automated Linear ↔ Beads bidirectional sync
description: Bidirectional automated sync between Linear and Beads. Linear webhook triggers bd operations on issue events. bd operations update Linear via MCP. Session-start sync resolves overnight drift. Assignee auto-set on claim.

title: KEI-21 — bd resume self-assign hook on [READY:] emission
description: On every \[READY:\] emission, hook automatically fires: bd ready --json | first-unblocked | bd update --claim. If claim succeeds → agent starts next task immediately. Mechanical self-assign — not instructional. Closes idle-agent gap at task completion.

title: KEI-20 — Better Stack severity-based alert routing
description: Configure Better Stack notification policy: critical incidents (service down, heartbeat missed) → #ceo. Resolved + routine heartbeat notifications → #execution. One PR. Aiden owns.

title: KEI-19 — Rate limit alerting: auto-post to #ceo on agent throttle
description: Add Anthropic rate-limit detection to the polling loop. When any agent hits a rate limit, immediately post to #ceo with callsign, wait duration, and auto-clear notification when they resume. Dave should never have to manually check to find throttled agents.

title: Elliot orchestrator polling loop — Haiku monitor, Sonnet escalation
description:
Elliot polling loop running on Haiku. TWO systemd timers:

* Every 1 min, 07:00–21:00 AEST
* Every 60 min, 21:00–07:00 AEST

Polling checks (Haiku only — no decisions):

1. Linear: In Progress issues with no activity >30 min → surface to #ceo
2. Beads: `bd ready` → dispatch newly unblocked work
3. Agent inboxes: idle agents → assign next item
4. Prefect: failed flows → create KEI pipeline-incident

Escalation: decision required → spawn Elliot session on Sonnet.

PRIORITY ELEVATED — ships before Cognee Phase 1.
close_reason: KEI-17 polling loop shipped + merged via PR #774 06fe5dd6; LAW XV bundled in Wave 2 priority-reset save fa00e83e.

title: KEI-44 — Cognee 3GB memory cap
description: Mirror of Linear KEI-44. Containment cap on cognee_ingest.py + cognee.api.client subprocesses. Implementation: ulimit -v / systemd-run --scope MemoryMax=3G / cgroup. Verifiable via /proc/<pid>/limits. Blocks Cognee resume; parallel to KEI-43.
acceptance_criteria: Process memory hard-capped at 3GB measurable via /proc/<pid>/limits or systemctl scope; reproducible test with 10-chunk batch; runbook entry for resume procedure.
notes: Aiden routing recommendation: Orion (Cognee context). Atlas fallback. Dispatch via Aiden, Orion is Aiden's clone.

title: KEI-43 — agent auto-start systemd services
description: Mirror of Linear KEI-43. One systemd --user service per agent (elliot/aiden/max/atlas/orion/scout). Each tmux-new-session + agent_session_launcher.sh + WantedBy=default.target + Restart=on-failure. Reference: openclaw.service. Ships before Cognee resume per Dave 2026-05-13 23:50.
acceptance_criteria: 6 systemd user units checked in; each enabled via systemctl --user enable; reboot test → all 6 agents online within 2min, no Dave touch.
notes: Atlas dispatch (engineering, Elliot's clone). Coordinate with Aiden on overlap vs orion/agency-os-34s-systemd-installer branch.

title: KEI-40 — slack_relay.py rate limit handling: exponential backoff + Retry-After respect
description:
## Problem

`slack_relay.py` is hitting Slack API rate limits during burst posting periods. Error: "Server is temporarily limiting requests (not your usage limit) · Rate limited."

Currently the relay has no rate limit handling — it fires requests immediately and fails silently or errors out when rate limited.

## Impact

Message delivery failures during peak governance activity (multiple agents posting simultaneously). Creates appearance of broken communication when underlying work is proceeding correctly.

## Fix

Add exponential backoff with retry to `slack_relay.py`:

```python
import time

def post_with_backoff(client, channel, message, max_retries=4):
    delay = 1
    for attempt in range(max_retries):
        response = client.chat_postMessage(channel=channel, text=message)
        if response.get("ok"):
            return response
        if response.get("error") == "ratelimited":
            retry_after = int(response.headers.get("Retry-After", delay))
            time.sleep(retry_after)
            delay *= 2
        else:
            raise Exception(response.get("error"))
    raise Exception("Max retries exceeded")
```

Also: respect `Retry-After` header from Slack when present.

## Acceptance criteria

* No silent message drops during burst posting
* Rate limit errors logged but retried automatically
* `Retry-After` header respected

## Owner

Aiden — post Stream 3+4 completion, before Elliot restart.

title: KEI-36 — Fix HEARTBEAT.md placeholder population in pre-compact hook
description:
## Problem

`scripts/pre_compact_alert.py` snapshots HEARTBEAT.md before context compaction. When agents fail to fill the template manually, the snapshot contains raw placeholders: `<directive number or label>`, `<one-line>`, `<git short-sha>`, etc. The anchor is useless for post-compact session recovery.

Observed in production: two consecutive PRE-COMPACT alerts today showed blank template fields across Elliot's session.

## Fix — Two parts

### Part 1: Auto-populate from mechanical sources

Before snapshotting, `pre_compact_alert.py` should detect placeholder text (grep for `<` followed by known field names) and auto-fill:

* **Last Good Commit SHA + Subject** → `git log -1 --format='%h %s' HEAD`
* **Branch** → `git branch --show-current`
* **Model (Configured)** → read callsign from IDENTITY.md, look up in CLAUDE.md model table
* **Active Task / Goal** → `bd ready` or most recent Linear In Progress issue for this agent
* **Blockers** → `bd list --blocked` for current worktree
* **Next Action** → derive from current branch name + last commit subject if no explicit entry

No LLM call required. Pure mechanical sources.

### Part 2: Validate before snapshot

After auto-fill attempt, scan for remaining placeholders. If any found:

* Post warning to #execution: `[HEARTBEAT-INCOMPLETE] callsign=X — N fields still contain placeholders`
* Still proceed with compaction (don't block) but flag clearly
* Never silently snapshot a blank template

## Acceptance criteria

* Pre-compact snapshot of any agent HEARTBEAT.md contains zero `<placeholder>` fields
* Warning posted to #execution when fields cannot be auto-resolved
* All 6 agent worktrees covered

## Owner

Aiden — extend `scripts/pre_compact_alert.py`. Branch off main. Standard dual-CTO concur before self-merge.

title: Agent session recovery — internal mechanical pattern
description: Dave CEO directive ts ~1778647600: Dead agent tmux sessions are internal recovery tasks. Machine detects, restarts, reports in activity log. Never surface to Dave unless two recovery attempts fail. Pattern lives in ceo_memory key orchestration:agent_session_recovery + this Beads issue for future-deployment inheritance. Recovery path: tmux new-session + claude --resume + option 1 summary + brief incoming agent. Two-attempt threshold before escalation. Same class as stream-crash + concurrency-bug + retry-decision protocol (Dave ts ~1778642820).

title: KEI-35 — Automatic agent session recovery (dead tmux detection + restart)
description:
## Problem

When an agent's tmux session dies (crash, OOM, detached process taking it down), the machine currently surfaces this to Dave as an operator action. This is wrong. It is an internal mechanical operation.

## Required behaviour

1. Polling loop detects dead agent session (tmux session missing or unresponsive)
2. Machine restarts the session automatically — no human involvement
3. Agent re-reads IDENTITY.md, HEARTBEAT.md, reconnects to Slack, resumes work
4. Activity log records: `[RECOVERY] Max session restarted at HH:MM — cause: tmux session lost`
5. Only surfaces to #ceo if two recovery attempts fail

## Beads memory pattern to encode

`agent_session_recovery = internal_mechanical`

Every Keiracom deployment inherits this behaviour. Dead sessions restart automatically. This is foundational to the machine being self-healing.

## Implementation

* Add tmux session liveness check to polling loop (alongside existing subprocess detection)
* If session missing: `tmux new-session -d -s [agent_name]` + reinject IDENTITY context
* If session exists but unresponsive: kill + restart
* Log to ceo_memory + activity feed
* Two-failure gate before #ceo escalation

## Why this matters for the Keiracom product

This is the core self-healing behaviour that makes Keiracom production-grade. Users of the bundle should never restart an agent manually.

title: Polling loop AttributeError on Linear-stale path — assignee=null crash
description: Post Agency_OS-yvz fix: polling loop now progresses past bd_ready/asyncpg to Linear-stale path, where it crashes on AttributeError. Line 265 compose_dispatches: it.get('assignee', {}).get('name', '?') — but Linear returns 'assignee: null' (not missing key), so it.get('assignee', {}) returns None not {}, and .get('name') on None crashes. Fix: use it.get('assignee') or {} pattern instead. Trivial 1-line. Scout's prior diagnosis missed this because bd_ready=0 short-circuited the Linear path before it could crash. Now manifesting.

title: Polling loop bd_ready returns 0 + idle detection broken — KEI-17 regression

Plus Prefect query gets 404 (wrong URL?). Three bugs in one PR likely needs decomposition: (a) bd ready --json invocation in poll_bd_ready() failing or wrong cwd; (b) poll_idle_agents() threshold/path bug; (c) Prefect endpoint URL config bug. Real-world finding: mechanical idle dispatch isn't firing despite the hook being installed. Audit-Pattern-A territory: 'shipped without empirical end-to-end' check.

title: KEI-40 — slack_relay.py: exponential backoff on Slack rate-limit (429)
description:
Per Dave verbatim ts ~1778666400. Today's session repeatedly hit Slack chat.postMessage rate-limits. slack_relay.py exits non-zero on URLError without retry, generating governance noise that makes working agents look stuck.

Fix: add exponential-backoff retry loop in scripts/slack_relay.py's post() function. On HTTP 429 (or urllib URLError with HTTPError.code == 429), parse retry-after header if present, else exponential delay: 1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s capped at 30s. Max 5 retries before final failure.

Ownership open per Elliot ts ~1778666440: Orion folds into KEI-22 if scope-aligned (Linear-Beads sync is the closest infra surface), else Aiden post-hygiene-sweep. Either path acceptable; this issue queues for whichever is faster.

Acceptance: slack_relay.py post() survives 1-2 transient 429s without governance noise. Final failure (5x exhausted) still surfaces to stderr.

title: Cognee Lance cross-process writer serialisation (fcntl flock / DB advisory lock)
description: Per Max correction ts ~1778661715 on Aiden's PR #826 scope catch: PR #826's asyncio.Semaphore(1) wrapper around LanceDBAdapter.index_data_points is PROCESS-LOCAL (module-level state in src/cognee/client.py). It correctly fixes the INTRA-process race that killed Stream 2 at 2026-05-13T01:54:53 (cognee's internal task scheduler firing concurrent asyncio.gather batches within one Python process). It does NOT cover the cross-process race: two Python processes each calling cognify() against the same Lance dataset would each hold their own semaphore and could re-trigger the lance retry_timeout Too-many-concurrent-writers failure. Discipline (one ingest at a time, per Resolution A coordination) avoids this in practice, but defense-in-depth is sound: extend the monkeypatch in src/cognee/client.py to acquire an fcntl flock (or DB advisory lock if Lance exposes one) on a stable lockfile path (e.g. /home/elliotbot/.cognee_system/lance.lock) before entering the asyncio.Semaphore region. Falls through to non-blocking try-acquire on collision so the second process surfaces a clean error instead of looping. P2 — non-urgent given coordination discipline; bumps to P1 if any cross-process collision is observed in the wild.

title: Cognee Lance writer cross-process serialisation (fcntl/DB lock around LanceDBAdapter)
description: PR #826 asyncio.Semaphore(1) in src/cognee/client.py module state is PROCESS-LOCAL. Two Python processes both calling cognify() against the same Lance dataset would each hold their own semaphore, re-triggering the cross-process Lance writer-conflict (Too many concurrent writers). Defense-in-depth: add fcntl.flock or DB advisory lock around LanceDBAdapter.index_data_points so cross-process writes serialise too. Lower priority while discipline (one ingest at a time) holds. Surfaced by Aiden during Stream 3+4 Resolution-A coordination 2026-05-13.
close_reason: Superseded by Agency_OS-nk8 (Aiden filed canonical version with fcntl flock path + non-blocking try-acquire detail, per Max preference ts ~1778661820).

Venv recreate / pip --force-reinstall would wipe ingest state. After Stream 2/3/4 ingest completes and VQs pass, move to /home/elliotbot/clawd/cognee_data/ alongside cognee_db SQLite. Likely requires Cognee env var DATA_ROOT_DIRECTORY or similar — current DB_PATH only points cognee_db SQLite, not Lance system dir.

title: Self-assign hook needs role-filter for clones (scout/atlas/orion auto-claim wrong scope)
description: Self-assign hook on [READY:<callsign>] emits has no role-scope filter. Two empirical false-positives this session: (1) scout's [READY:scout] auto-claimed Agency_OS-dhe (Cognee Phase 2 P0); (2) scout's [READY:scout] auto-claimed Agency_OS-yvz (polling loop fix P1 — Aiden's). Clones should only auto-claim items matching their role-scope (research, mem0-audit, KEI-11-style outcomes), not primary build PRs. Fix options: (A) skip-claim entirely for clone callsigns (atlas/orion/scout) — fall back to polling-loop dispatch; (B) role-allowed labels on Beads issues + hook filters via label match. (A) is simpler. Pair with Stop-hook merge for the matching fix shape.
close_reason: Clone-callsign role-filter shipped + merged via PR #791 60b0a413. Self-assign hook now skips claim for atlas/orion/scout; polling-loop is the canonical dispatch path for clones (post Agency_OS-yvz fix).

title: Better Stack supabase-rest monitor — 401 false-incident, needs auth header config
description: supabase-rest monitor (id 4400118) is firing incidents because Supabase REST root returns 401 (no auth) instead of 200. Better Stack default expects 2xx. Fix options: (a) configure monitor with auth header (Authorization: Bearer SUPABASE_ANON_KEY); (b) point monitor at a public-no-auth endpoint like /rest/v1/?select=count or /auth/v1/health; (c) accept-401-as-OK if Better Stack supports custom expected_status. First incident fired immediately on monitor create.

title: systemd-shipping PRs need post-merge install step (PR #774 gap)
description: PR #774 (KEI-17 polling loop) shipped systemd unit files into repo systemd/ but no install step into ~/.config/systemd/user/. Result: timer not running until manual cp + daemon-reload + enable. Pattern A risk (writer-side shipped without reader-side enablement). Fix: future PRs touching systemd/ require either (a) post-merge install script auto-run via CI/hook, or (b) explicit install steps in PR description checked at merge gate. Same gap-class as Aiden's KEI-12 cleanup-timer would have hit if we'd not caught it.

title: Cross-worktree Beads DB sync — CTOs can't run bd locally until resolved
description: Aiden's PR #771 review observation #2: 'Use bd for ALL task tracking' rule across worktrees doesn't work today because the Beads embedded Dolt DB is per-worktree, not shared. Pragmatic interim: TaskCreate locally for CTOs/clones until cross-worktree Beads sync solved (per upstream's refs/dolt/data git-sync model).

title: BS monitor: distinguish cognify completion-exit from crash-exit
description: Per Elliot ts ~1778654958 + Aiden confirm during Stream 2 seal: Better Stack incident alert fires on cognee-phase1-ingest when the process exits cleanly (cognify() complete → done: 1678 ok / 0 failed → process exits → heartbeat stops). Monitor sees no-heartbeat and flags incident. False-positive — completion is the goal state, not a failure mode. Fix candidates: (a) BS monitor tuned to accept heartbeat-stops-after-completion-marker; (b) wrap cognee_ingest.py to fire a 'completion' heartbeat to a separate BS endpoint on success path; (c) BS monitor flapping threshold tuned per-job-type (long-running batch jobs vs daemons have different no-heartbeat semantics). P3 — non-urgent (humans can dismiss false alert), but adds noise to alerting signal-to-noise.

title: KEI-36 v2 — wire mcp__memory lookup to existing ceo_memory orchestration:model_assignment
description: Per Elliot scope correction ts ~1778654710: ceo_memory key orchestration:model_assignment IS ALREADY POPULATED with structured data (Elliot orchestrator write ts ~1778625870). Structure verified: primaries_unchanged (elliot/aiden/max opus-4-7) + clones_switched (atlas/orion/scout sonnet-4-6, scout also haiku-4-5 mechanical). Task is WIRING, not population: extend pre_compact_alert.py auto_populate_heartbeat() to query mcp__memory__get_by_id (or equivalent direct supabase read) for the key, parse the structured payload, and populate the Configured model field deterministically based on resolved callsign. Once wired, the [HEARTBEAT-INCOMPLETE] warning will stop firing for the Configured field. Running field still surfaces via warning (no --model flag introspection from inside hook process — separate gap).

title: Better Stack agencyxos.ai monitor — fix 422 redirect/cookie config
description: PR #786 uptime monitor creation for agencyxos.ai failed with HTTP 422: 'Cannot follow redirects when expecting a 3xx status code; Cannot keep cookies when redirecting when expecting a 3xx status code'. Vercel-deployed site likely returns 308 redirect to www. Fix: configure monitor with expected_status_code=308 OR follow_redirects=false OR use the canonical URL that doesn't redirect (try www.agencyxos.ai or .vercel.app). Small Aiden follow-up.

title: Get familiar with Linear
description:
Welcome to Linear! 

Watch an introductory video and access a list of resources below.

[LinearH264Version_1.mp4](https://uploads.linear.app/fe63b3e2-bf87-46c0-8784-cd7d639287c8/a044fb03-9b84-470c-ab6f-8eae613e2529/98d7274d-de7f-4910-b3f3-f72e8e286a98)

### **Choose your setup guide** based on your company stage:

* [Small teams](https://linear.app/docs/how-to-use-linear-small-teams)
  For early-stage startups and projects
* [Startups & mid-size companies](https://linear.app/docs/how-to-use-linear-startups-mid-size-companies)
  For growing teams with cross-functional needs
* [Large & scaling companies](https://linear.app/docs/how-to-use-linear-large-scaling-companies)
  For enterprise and high-growth teams with complex workflows

### **Need help getting started?**

* [Join our Slack community](https://linear.app/join-slack)
  Connect with other Linear users and get tips
* [Join a live ](https://lu.ma/welcome-to-linear?utm_source=docs)[onboarding](https://lu.ma/welcome-to-linear?utm_source=onboarding)[ ](https://lu.ma/welcome-to-linear?utm_source=docs)[session](https://lu.ma/welcome-to-linear?utm_source=onboarding)
  Learn the essentials and see demos of core workflows

---

If you have any questions hit `?` in the bottom left > Contact us.

![contactlinear (1).gif](https://uploads.linear.app/fe63b3e2-bf87-46c0-8784-cd7d639287c8/bc9bbf62-4192-411f-88f6-c89c9150503e/4df0346e-803b-4f58-8527-4aeb30d88411)

title: Connect your tools
description:
Integrations turn Linear into your source of truth around product development. Keep data in sync, and eliminate manual updates between tools.

![connect-your-tools.png](https://uploads.linear.app/fe63b3e2-bf87-46c0-8784-cd7d639287c8/c2eae035-37e2-4754-adcb-b8305431aa1f/c92d70c7-e6d0-4fa2-a0fd-78f6e780993a)

### **Key integrations**

* [**Slack**](<https://linear.app/settings/integrations/slack>)
  Create issues from Slack messages and sync threads
* [**GitHub**](<https://linear.app/settings/integrations/github>)** / **[**GitLab**](<https://linear.app/settings/integrations/gitlab>)
  Automate your pull request, commit workflows, and keep issues synced both ways
* [**Agents**](<https://linear.app/integrations/agents>)
  Deploy AI agents that work alongside you as teammates

### **Browse all integrations**

Discover 150+ available connections in our [integration directory](<https://linear.app/integrations>) – from bug creation via support tools (Intercom, Zendesk), to issues created from design explorations (Figma).

### **Linear API**

If you need something more custom, you can build directly on the Linear API (built on GraphQL). [See our Dev Docs to learn more](<https://linear.app/developers>).

title: Set up your teams
description:
This workspace is a container for your organization’s work. 

* [Learn more about Workspaces](https://linear.app/docs/workspaces)
  How to configure settings and workflows 

Teams are how you organize people and work in Linear.

* [Learn about Teams](https://linear.app/docs/teams)
  How to structure teams and configure workflows

Teams are made of members with defined roles (Admin, Member, Guest).

* [Learn more about Members](https://linear.app/docs/invite-members)
  Add your team and assign roles

---

Ready to add your team? Invite via CSV or a unique link in [settings](http://linear.app/settings/members).
